entries.name: Positional Offensive Pressure
entries.text: archetype=attack | pattern=improve position to enable effective direct pressure on a target | trigger=when a target is vulnerable but success depends on alignment, access, or maintaining attacker safety | strategy=maneuver into advantageous range or side, then apply controlled offensive force while preserving continued action | games=diplomacy,pokemon_red,sokoban
entries.description: Create or exploit an advantageous position to apply direct pressure against a target while preserving your ability to continue the attack. The pattern centers on maneuvering into effective striking alignment before committing force.

entries.name: Pressure Russia North
entries.text: game=diplomacy | skill=Pressure Russia North | effects=attack_completed | context=use when attacking Russia by leveraging Galicia and Bohemia while maintaining Greece-Trieste defensive coverage
entries.description: Push into Russia’s northern holdings by coordinating pressure on St. Petersburg and nearby seas, often with northern allies while Austria advances elsewhere. Use it when Russia is overextended or tied

entries.name: Win the Battle
entries.text: game=pokemon_red | skill=Win the Battle | effects=attack_completed | context=use during any active battle to keep attacking until the opposing Pokemon faints, including rival trainer and wild encounters
entries.description: Use a damaging move in battle to lower the opposing Pokémon’s HP and eventually knock it out. Use this on your turn whenever a fight starts or continues and you want to win the battle in Pokémon Red.

entries.name: Preserve Squirtle Safely
entries.text: game=pokemon_red | skill=Preserve Squirtle Safely | effects=survive_completed | context=use in wild battles when Squirtle is low HP to heal immediately, safely finish the fight, or flee while conserving health and resources
entries.description: Keep Squirtle alive by switching it out before HP gets dangerously low and using safer teammates or items to finish fights. Use this whenever Squirtle is threatened by strong attacks, bad matchups, or

entries.name: Open Box Route
entries.text: game=sokoban | skill=Open Box Route | effects=attack_completed | context=use when attacking by pushing a box off a wall or along a column to free its path toward the target area
entries.description: Moves the worker around an accessible side of a box to get behind it, opening a valid push route. Use it in Sokoban when a box is reachable but blocked from the needed pushing direction.

entries.name: Secure Base Expansion
entries.text: archetype=build | pattern=establish secure foundation before expanding | trigger=when a safe opening, anchor, or protected space is available | strategy=stabilize core structure first, then grow around it to preserve flexibility and reduce risk | games=diplomacy,tetris,twenty_forty_eight
entries.description: Build from a stable, low-risk foundation before extending outward. When you have protected space or a safe anchor, use it to reinforce position and enable stronger future growth.

entries.name: Fortify the Frontier
entries.text: game=diplomacy | skill=Fortify the Frontier | effects=build_completed | context=use during build phase to place a defensive unit in Trieste or Budapest, securing key borders and deterring Italy, Turkey, or Russia pressure
entries.description: Use this build when Austria has an open home center after the winter adjustments to strengthen its borders. Add an army in Vienna or Trieste, or a fleet in Trieste, to cover key fronts against Italy,

entries.name: Keep Stack Low
entries.text: game=tetris | skill=Keep Stack Low | effects=setup_completed | context=setup phase; place L/S/O flat and tuck T into left or left-center notch to smooth the surface, preserve the I slot, and stay flexible for upcoming pieces like O, I, S, L, T, Z
entries.description: In Tetris, keep the stack flat and low by placing early pieces to fill shallow gaps instead of building towers. Use this in the opening and whenever the board is clean to preserve space and flexibilit

entries.name: Stabilize Corner Stack
entries.text: game=twenty_forty_eight | skill=Stabilize Corner Stack | effects=setup_completed | context=use during setup to keep the highest tile cornered, preserve empty cells, compress safely, and align near-term merges without breaking the anchor
entries.description: Keep your highest tile locked in a corner and build a descending row/column next to it (e.g., 32-16-8-4), using only the two “safe” directions that preserve that corner. Use this once your corner is e

entries.name: Immediate Path Clearing
entries.text: archetype=clear | pattern=remove immediate blockage or reset to a clean usable state | trigger=when a direct action can instantly clear space, restore access, or eliminate a local obstacle | strategy=take the simplest available move that immediately clears, heals, or flattens the situation to enable smoother follow-up play | games=avalon,candy_crush,pokemon_red,sokoban,super_mario,tetris
entries.description: Use a readily available action to remove an immediate obstacle, resolve a blocking condition, or restore a clear workable state. This pattern favors simple, low-risk cleanup that opens movement, progress, or future options right away.

entries.name: Read Team Motives
entries.text: game=avalon | skill=Read Team Motives | effects=clear_completed | context=use when tag_clear events require inferring Player 0 alignment from opening proposals, quest team choices, approval votes, and inconsistent supporters to confirm trustworthy teams
entries.description: Notice who consistently proposes or supports teams that give their side the best odds, especially around early Quest 1 and after mission results. Use this to infer hidden loyalties by comparing stated

entries.name: Make Immediate Match
entries.text: game=candy_crush | skill=Make Immediate Match | effects=attack_completed | context=use on tag_attack to make the strongest immediate 3-match for quick points and possible cascades while preserving follow-up options
entries.description: Swap adjacent candies to complete a match immediately, clearing pieces and scoring right away. Use it whenever a simple 3+ match is available, especially early in the board to keep moves efficient and

entries.name: Clear Immediate Match
entries.text: game=candy_crush | skill=clear_immediate_match | effects=clear_completed | context=use when an immediate 3-match is available to raise score, reduce remaining candies, and improve cascade potential
entries.description: Swap two adjacent candies to complete an immediate 3-in-a-row and clear it. Use this whenever a direct match is available, especially early in the board, to score points and open space for new candies

entries.name: Make Immediate Match
entries.text: game=candy_crush | skill=Make Immediate Match | effects=execute_completed | context=use when a swap creates an immediate 3-candy row or column match for quick points while preserving future board flexibility
entries.description: Swap two adjacent candies to complete a 3-in-a-row match immediately. Use it when an obvious match is available, especially early in the level, to score points, clear space, and set up cascades.

entries.name: Heal at Pokecenter
entries.text: game=pokemon_red | skill=Heal at Pokecenter | effects=clear_completed | context=use after a clear milestone or battle win to restore HP and PP at a Pokemon Center before continuing the route
entries.description: Use a Pokémon Center to fully heal your party’s HP and cure status conditions by talking to Nurse Joy and choosing to heal. Do this after battles, before caves/routes, or anytime your team is weakened

entries.name: Probe Corridor Access
entries.text: game=sokoban | skill=Probe Corridor Access | effects=explore_completed | context=use during exploration to test corridor routes toward a trapped box and avoid repeated side-to-side oscillation
entries.description: Moves the worker down narrow hallways and dead-end passages to check whether they connect to useful pushing positions or trap routes. Use it when the remaining box’s path is unclear and you need to re

entries.name: Clear Pipe Cleanly
entries.text: game=super_mario | skill=Clear Pipe Cleanly | effects=clear_completed | context=use when approaching a pipe while moving right; jump over or onto the pipe, avoid nearby Goombas, and preserve momentum to continue safely
entries.description: Run through a clear pipe smoothly by entering at full speed and staying aligned so Mario doesn’t bump or lose momentum. Use it when a transparent pipe bridges a gap or leads past enemies to keep the r

entries.name: Seal and Flatten
entries.text: game=tetris | skill=Seal and Flatten | effects=clear_completed | context=use on tag_clear turns to drop into center-right gaps, seal holes, avoid overhangs, and flatten the stack for smooth follow-ups
entries.description: Place the current piece to complete one or more full rows, clearing them and leaving the surface flatter. Use it whenever you can remove lines without creating overhangs or new holes, especially early

entries.name: Smooth the Surface
entries.text: game=tetris | skill=Smooth the Surface | effects=flatten contour, reduce holes, avoid overhangs, preserve flexibility | context=use when placing J/S/T to fill valleys or cavities, smooth center or side surfaces, and keep wells and future piece options open
entries.description: Flatten uneven columns by placing pieces to fill dips and reduce jagged height differences, even if it doesn’t clear lines immediately. Use it in midgame when the stack is moderate and holes exist, so

entries.name: Place for flatness
entries.text: game=tetris | skill=Place for flatness | effects=position_completed | context=use when positioning pieces to flatten the center or left-center surface, fill notches/valleys, avoid creating holes, and preserve flexible future clears
entries.description: Place pieces to keep the surface as flat and even as possible, avoiding tall spikes or deep wells. Use this on routine placements in Tetris, especially early or when no line clear/setup is available,

entries.name: Clear Safely
entries.text: game=twenty_forty_eight | skill=Clear Safely | effects=clear_completed | context=use when needing to open space with safe merges while preserving the anchored high tile and keeping rows flexible
entries.description: Use this when the board is mostly stable and your highest tile is anchored in a corner: make the safe sweep move that clears a matched pair without breaking your snake/monotonic order. It opens space

entries.name: Claim Early Resource Nodes
entries.text: archetype=collect | pattern=claim valuable resources from accessible interactive spots | trigger=when a new resource source or one-time pickup becomes available early | strategy=interact immediately with fixed opportunity points to secure items or advantages for future progress | games=pokemon_red, super_mario
entries.description: Identify interactive points that yield valuable items or advantages, then collect from them as soon as they become available. This pattern emphasizes early acquisition of resources from fixed opportunities to strengthen future progress.

entries.name: Take Starter Pokémon
entries.text: game=pokemon_red | skill=Take Starter Pokémon | effects=collect_completed | context=use in Oak's Lab at the start of the game to interact with a Poké Ball and receive your first Pokémon from Professor Oak
entries.description: Choose your first Pokémon from Professor Oak’s table in Pallet Town after he stops you from leaving town. Use this at the start of Pokémon Red to receive Bulbasaur, Charmander, or Squirtle and begin t

entries.name: Hit Question Blocks
entries.text: game=super_mario | skill=hit_question_blocks | effects=collect_completed | context=use when a nearby overhead question block appears while moving right; jump to hit it and collect before continuing
entries.description: Jump underneath question-mark blocks in Super Mario to hit them with Mario’s head and release their item or coins. Use this when a block is directly above Mario or reachable from a nearby platform.

entries.name: Preemptive Position Denial
entries.text: archetype=defend | pattern=preemptively deny threats by securing vulnerable space or challenging risky openings | trigger=when an opponent can exploit an exposed position, suspicious setup, or structural gap | strategy=fortify key points and interrupt developing advantages before they become dangerous | games=avalon,diplomacy,super_mario,tetris
entries.description: Anticipate a likely threat or vulnerability and act early to block the opponent’s access, momentum, or advantage. The pattern emphasizes protective positioning and timely interference rather than direct aggression.

entries.name: Challenge Team Approval
entries.text: game=avalon | skill=Challenge Team Approval | effects=defend_completed | context=use on tag_defend to reject a risky quest-four team by questioning inconsistent approvers and protecting the loyal majority
entries.description: Challenge a proposed team approval when the lineup includes players with suspicious voting or quest histories, especially in late-game quests where one fail can decide the match. Use it as a Servant t

entries.name: Secure Defensive Line
entries.text: game=diplomacy | skill=Secure Defensive Line | effects=defend_completed | context=use when defending key centers, choosing safe retreats, and preserving flexible coverage against advancing neighbors without blocking home centers
entries.description: Form a tight Austrian defensive shell—typically holding Vienna/Serbia while fleets anchor Greece/Trieste—to cover home centers and key approaches. Use it when pressured by Italy, Turkey, or Russia, es

entries.name: Stomp Under Block
entries.text: game=super_mario | skill=Stomp Under Block | effects=defend_completed | context=use when defending against a Goomba beneath a low overhead block; time a safe jump to stomp without hitting the block
entries.description: Jump up from directly beneath a question block or brick to hit it from below, making the block bounce and knocking out or disabling enemies standing on top. Use this when a Goomba or Koopa is walking

entries.name: Tuck L Left
entries.text: game=tetris | skill=Tuck L Left | effects=defend_completed | context=use on defend when an L piece can fill the left or left-center valley/cavity to avoid new holes, reduce existing holes, and stabilize the stack
entries.description: Rotate and slide the L piece left to tuck it under an overhang, plugging a left-side cavity without raising the stack. Use it in midgame defense when the board has left-facing gaps and you need to red

entries.name: Finalize Required Setup
entries.text: archetype=execute | pattern=complete final prerequisite step to unlock progression | trigger=when progress is blocked by an unfinished intro, setup, or position-dependent requirement | strategy=identify the remaining required state and perform the exact final interaction or movement to satisfy it | games=pokemon_red,sokoban
entries.description: Complete the immediate prerequisite sequence that unlocks progress by performing the last required positioning or interaction step. This pattern applies when a short scripted or spatial setup must be cleanly finished before the main task can begin.

entries.name: Finish Oak Lab Start
entries.text: game=pokemon_red | skill=Finish Oak Lab Start | effects=execute_completed | context=use to advance Oak's Lab opening after choosing and naming Squirtle, talk to Blue, complete the first rival battle, and finish post-battle intro dialogue before leaving
entries.description: Completes the final Oak’s Lab intro sequence in Pokémon Red: receive your starter, finish the first rival battle, and exit the lab. Use it right after choosing a Pokémon when the opening lab events ar

entries.name: Push Box Onto Target
entries.text: game=sokoban | skill=push box onto target | effects=execute_completed | context=use when a box is aligned for a final push into a target square, especially pushing up or left into an open target
entries.description: Push a box one square onto a target by moving the worker behind it and shoving it forward. Use this when a box is lined up with a target and the destination square is open, without trapping the box.

entries.name: Opening Area Scouting
entries.text: archetype=explore | pattern=systematically scout the initial accessible region to reveal map knowledge and nearby opportunities | trigger=when entering a new game or starting zone with limited information | strategy=cover surrounding paths and hubs early to identify resources, threats, and progression routes before committing | games=pokemon_red
entries.description: Early in a game, the player surveys the immediately accessible starting region to uncover layout, resources, encounters, and nearby progression paths. This builds foundational knowledge that improves routing, preparation, and decision-making for the

entries.name: Explore Opening Area
entries.text: game=pokemon_red | skill=Explore Opening Area | effects=explore_completed | context=use when starting in Pallet Town to inspect Oak's Lab, talk to nearby NPCs, check notable objects, and head north on Route 1 toward Viridian City to find the next progression point
entries.description: Explores the opening routes and towns around Pallet Town/Viridian to reveal tiles, enter buildings, talk to NPCs, and spot items or exits. Use it early in Pokémon Red after gaining control, before com

entries.name: Anchored Merge Cascade
entries.text: archetype=merge | pattern=combine matching elements while preserving a fixed anchor for the strongest piece | trigger=when progress depends on repeated merges and board stability matters | strategy=lock the highest-value asset in a safe edge or corner, preserve adjacent space, and funnel new merges toward it in order | games=twenty_forty_eight
entries.description: Consolidate matching resources by repeatedly combining like elements while maintaining a stable anchor position that protects your strongest asset. Build outward from that anchor so merges remain organized, predictable, and low-risk.

entries.name: Merge Matching Tiles
entries.text: game=twenty_forty_eight | skill=Merge Matching Tiles | effects=merge_completed, tag_merge | context=use when adjacent equal tiles can be combined while keeping the board consolidated around an anchored corner tile
entries.description: Slide the board so two tiles with the same number collide and combine into one higher-value tile, like 2+2=4. Use this whenever matching tiles can merge, especially early on to create space and grow y

entries.name: Anchor High Tile
entries.text: game=twenty_forty_eight | skill=Anchor High Tile | effects=position_completed | context=use when positioning to keep the largest tile fixed in a corner, shift left to preserve open space, align small equal tiles, and set up safe early merges
entries.description: Keep your highest tile locked in one corner (usually a bottom corner) and avoid moves that pull it away. Use this from the opening onward so lower tiles can stack beside it in descending order for saf

entries.name: Preserve Anchor Space
entries.text: game=twenty_forty_eight | skill=Preserve Anchor Space | effects=survive_completed | context=use when board space is tight to keep the highest tile anchored in a corner, preserve empty cells near it, and force safe merges to avoid lockup
entries.description: Keep one corner as your permanent home for the largest tile and preserve an adjacent empty “anchor” cell so the top row/column stays stable. Use this in mid-to-late game, especially when the board is

entries.name: Safe Progressive Positioning
entries.text: archetype=navigate | pattern=incremental safe movement toward a goal state | trigger=when progress requires repositioning through constrained space without collision or misplacement | strategy=move stepwise, read nearby obstacles, and choose placements or paths that preserve safety while advancing | games=pokemon_red, super_mario, tetris
entries.description: Advance toward a target state through controlled movement while respecting immediate spatial constraints and avoiding costly mistakes. The pattern emphasizes incremental navigation, hazard awareness, and correct placement or pathing to enable future

entries.name: Start the Journey
entries.text: game=pokemon_red | skill=Start the Journey | effects=navigate_completed | context=use when leaving Red's bedroom and house, exiting Oak's Lab, and heading north from Pallet Town toward Viridian City to begin early-game progression
entries.description: Starts a new Pokémon Red game by advancing through the title screen and opening intro dialogue. Use it at the very beginning, from the startup/title screen, to reach the initial in-game opening sequen

entries.name: Move Right Safely
entries.text: game=super_mario | skill=Move Right Safely | effects=navigate_completed | context=advance right without jumping, line up beneath the nearer question block, stay safe as the Goomba approaches, continue under the block cluster
entries.description: Move Mario to the right while staying grounded and avoiding nearby hazards or bad jumps. Use this in the opening stretch or any safe lane to advance toward question blocks, enemies, and the next scree

entries.name: Fill S Gap
entries.text: game=tetris | skill=Fill S Gap | effects=navigate_completed | context=[NAVIGATE] Drop S into columns 3-5 gap quickly to preserve flexibility for T,Z,I,J
entries.description: Fill S Gap means guiding an S piece into a stepped notch—two adjacent cells offset by one row—to seal a small zigzag cavity. Use it when your stack leaves that exact S-shaped opening, especially in mi

entries.name: Early Irreversible-State Detection
entries.text: archetype=optimize | pattern=detect irreversible failure states before committing | trigger=when an action may trap resources or make goals unreachable | strategy=check for deadlocks and prune losing branches early | games=sokoban
entries.description: Identify actions that create unrecoverable or highly costly states before committing to them. Prune these doomed lines early to preserve resources and focus search on viable progress.

entries.name: Detect Deadlock Early
entries.text: game=sokoban | skill=detect_deadlock_early | effects=optimize_completed | context=use when a box is irretrievably stuck and the puzzle is unsolvable, to stop oscillating and end with minimal wasted moves
entries.description: Detects deadlocks early by checking whether a pushed box is trapped against walls, in corners, or blocking narrow corridors without reaching a goal. Use it before every push, especially in endgames, t

entries.name: Positional Setup and Access
entries.text: archetype=position | pattern=establish exact spatial arrangement or access before executing the decisive move | trigger=when outcomes depend on being on the correct tile, beside the correct object, or controlling key locations | strategy=first secure the required position or alignment, then act only once the configuration enables progress without avoidable risk | games=avalon,candy_crush,diplomacy
entries.description: This pattern is about recognizing that success depends on getting the right pieces, units, or agent into an exact location or configuration before the main action can work. It emphasizes spatial access, safe approach routes, and verifying that positi

entries.name: Assess Team Alignment
entries.text: game=avalon | skill=Assess Team Alignment | effects=position_completed | context=use on tag_position to evaluate quest-two team proposals, read alignment from approvals, and shape a trusted quest-three team toward reliable allies
entries.description: Judge whether proposed teammates are likely all on the same side by comparing their votes, mission outcomes, and consistency with prior claims. Use it before approving teams in opening and midgame rou

entries.name: Swap Row 3 Center
entries.text: game=candy_crush | skill=Swap Row 3 Center | effects=position_completed | context=use when tagging the strongest immediate 3-match by swapping the center candies in row 3
entries.description: Swap the two center candies in row 3 to create an immediate 3-match or trigger a nearby cascade. Use it in midgame when that row’s middle controls multiple possible matches or can activate special can

entries.name: Secure Balkan Frontier
entries.text: game=diplomacy | skill=Secure Balkan Frontier | effects=position_completed | context=use when Austria should hold Greece and Serbia, cover Trieste, reinforce Balkans with army/fleet builds, and pressure Russia, Italy, or Turkey from Galicia/Bohemia while maintaining a defensive eastern frontier
entries.description: As Austria, secure the Balkans by taking and holding Serbia, Greece, and Albania while keeping Vienna covered and the Adriatic under control. Use this in 1901–1902 to stabilize your southern frontier

entries.name: Trigger Position Event
entries.text: game=pokemon_red | skill=Trigger Position Event | effects=position_completed | context=use when movement to a specific map location or NPC trigger is required to advance story or start a key event/battle
entries.description: Stand on the specific map tile or coordinate that triggers a hidden position-based event in Pokémon Red, such as advancing a scripted scene. Use it when you need to activate story progress or a locati

entries.name: Avoid Deadlock Pushes
entries.text: game=sokoban | skill=Avoid Deadlock Pushes | effects=defend_completed | context=use on tag_defend when a box is deadlocked or near a wall/corner; stop futile pushes, preserve worker mobility, and stay positioned to avoid making the box unsolvable
entries.description: Before pushing a box, check that the destination won’t trap it against a wall or in a corner unless that square is a goal. Use this in midgame when repositioning near edges so you preserve future push

entries.name: Reach Push Position
entries.text: game=sokoban | skill=Reach Push Position | effects=navigate_completed | context=navigate around boxes and walls to get behind/below/beside a target box and set up the next push toward the goal
entries.description: Move the worker to the exact square beside a box needed for the next push, without moving any boxes yet. Use this in Sokoban when setting up a planned push path, especially early in the level.

entries.name: Get Behind Box
entries.text: game=sokoban | skill=Get Behind Box | effects=position_completed | context=move to the safe pushing side of a box, typically below or beside it, to line up the next push toward a target
entries.description: Move the worker around to stand directly behind a box, on the side opposite the direction you want to push. Use this in Sokoban before any push, especially early on when setting up a box’s path.

entries.name: Reach Upper-Left Access
entries.text: game=sokoban | skill=Reach Upper-Left Access | effects=setup_completed | context=use during setup to reposition the worker toward the top opening or below the box so the box can be approached from its upper-left side and safely pushed downward
entries.description: Move the worker into the upper-left access route by navigating to the left side and then up around the box. Use this when you need top-left positioning to approach or push the box from below or the ri

entries.name: Hold Position Safely
entries.text: game=sokoban | skill=Hold Position Safely | effects=survive_completed | context=use when survival is tagged and pushing would cause useless oscillation; make a safe non-push move instead
entries.description: Keeps the worker on a safe floor tile without pushing the box into a wall or corner, preserving future moves. Use it in midgame when the path is tight and any push could trap the only box or block acc

entries.name: Move Under Block
entries.text: game=super_mario | skill=Move Under Block | effects=position_completed | context=move right to align directly beneath the shifted question block for an upcoming jump without jumping yet
entries.description: Move Mario so he is directly underneath a question block, lining up his position before jumping to hit it. Use this in the opening area when a nearby block is above Mario and slightly ahead.

entries.name: Foundational Opening Commitment
entries.text: archetype=setup | pattern=early defining opening choice that anchors later strategy and signals intentions | trigger=game start or first decision point | strategy=use the initial move or read of an initial move to set direction, gain structure, and infer future play | games=avalon, diplomacy, pokemon_red
entries.description: This pattern centers on making or interpreting a high-impact choice at the very start of play that shapes the strategic landscape for everything that follows. It leverages early commitments to establish position, reveal intentions, or secure a stable

entries.name: Read Opening Proposal
entries.text: game=avalon | skill=read opening proposal | effects=setup_completed | context=use during tag_setup to interpret Player 2's initial team [2,0] for alignment and intent signals
entries.description: Reads the very first team proposal of the game to spot early signals: who is being trusted, excluded, or trying to control the narrative. Use it in Avalon’s opening round, before any quest results exi

entries.name: Open Serbia Galicia
entries.text: game=diplomacy | skill=Open Serbia Galicia | effects=setup_completed | context=Use in setup when opening toward Serbia and Galicia while diplomatically containing Italy and Russia
entries.description: Austria opens with A Bud→Ser and A Vie→Gal, usually alongside F Tri→Alb or hold, to claim Serbia while contesting Galicia immediately. Use it when you want early pressure on Russia and a strong Balkan

entries.name: Start with Squirtle
entries.text: game=pokemon_red | skill=Start with Squirtle | effects=setup_completed | context=use during opening setup to enter Oak's Lab, choose and nickname Squirtle, receive the Pokédex, and heal at Viridian Pokecenter before heading north
entries.description: In Pokémon Red’s opening at Professor Oak’s lab, choose Squirtle as your starter from the Poké Ball table. Use this at the beginning of a new game when setting up a run that starts with Squirtle.

entries.name: Position Before Collapse
entries.text: archetype=survive | pattern=stabilize a deteriorating position before it snowballs | trigger=when the board/state is messy, fragile, or close to failure | strategy=prioritize cleanup, flattening, and risk reduction over advancement | games=tetris
entries.description: When the current state becomes unstable or error-prone, shift focus from progress to recovery. Reduce immediate danger by simplifying, stabilizing, and restoring a safer structure before resuming normal play.

entries.name: Stabilize the Stack
entries.text: game=tetris | skill=stabilize the stack | effects=survive_completed, tag_survive | context=use when survival is priority; place pieces flat or vertically to avoid overhangs, limit new holes, preserve I/T access, cap dangerous columns, and prevent topping out
entries.description: Use this when the stack is getting messy or hole-filled: place pieces to flatten peaks, cap exposed cavities, and preserve a clean vertical well for future I-pieces. Prioritize safe, low-risk placemen
